Fern Polrrrcnr- pRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gll-232g
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (gt6) 322-0886
November 3,2OlO
Gary Steube
P. O. Box 6848, pMB 24g
Big Bear Lake, California g}3l5
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-10-176
Dear Mr. Steube:
Re:
This letter is.il relqonse to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "A"t")] pt"ose note thai our advice is based solely
on the provisions of the Act. we thereiore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other
conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflicior ini"r.rt or Government code
Section 1090.
As a member of the Board of Directors of the Big Bear Airport District (the ,.District,,),
may you participant in decisions involving the District'Jpurchase or*uniy-o*neo property that
is adjacent to the airport if you lease a hangar from the District that is within 500 feet of the
boundary of the property?
QUESTToN
CONCLUSION
FACTS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions do not prohibit you from participating in these
decisions' For purposes of the Act, pioperty leased on a mortt -to-month basis is not considered
an interest in real property.
You are a director of the District (the "District") and wish to participate in the
consideration of a potential purchase of county-owned property at the upcoming December
meeting' The property is currently used as a park. You^lease a hangar on a month-to-month basis
from the Districr that is within 50b feet of the uorraury oiit e pu.k.
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code' unless otherwise indicated. ine .egutations of the Fair political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of rirle 2 of thi Califbrnia cuJ. utn"g,rlarions. All
regulatory ref'erenses are to Title 2, Division 6 of thi Califbrnia coae oi'negulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-10-176
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will .,perform their
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused Uy itreir own financial interests or the
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section g1001(b)., section g7100
prohibits any public official from making, irrti"ipoting in making, o. ott "r*ire using his or her
official position to influence a governmental deciiion i-n which the official has a financial
interest.
The commi::i"l has adopted-an eighrstep srandard analysis for deciding whether an
official has a disqualifying conflict of interist. fn.grr"ii", 1g700(b).) The general rule,
however, is that a conflict of interest exists *h"n"rr". u puuri. officiai makef a governmental
decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of his or her
financial interests.
Step 1. Are you a "public official" within the meaning of Section g7100?
Section 82048 defines a public official as, "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a
state or local government agency." As a board member oi tt. oistrict, *iri.r, i, a local
government agency, you are a public official.-Therefore, you may not make, participate in
making' or otherwise use your position to make, participate in making or influence any decisions
that will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effecr on a;y "f t;; economic
interests.
step 2' will you be making, participating in making or influencing a governmental
decision?
A public official-"makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting within the
authority of his or her o-ffi". or position, votes on a matter, obligates or commits his or her
agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual ag-reement on behalf of his or her
agency' (Regulation 18702.1.) A public officiai "participate, i, u gou"mmental decision,, when,
acting within the authority of his oi h". position ani without significant substantive or
intervening review, the official negotiatis, advises, or makes recommendations to the
decisionmaker regarding the governmental decision. 6eguration 1g702.2.) A public official is
attempting to use his orherofficial position to influent" igou"*.rental decision if, for the
purpose of influencing the decision, the official contacts o, uppro., before or otherwise attempts
to influence' any -".*f"., officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Regulation
18702'3') By considering the purchase oi the county "*r.a land, you tiir u"'*ur.ing and
participating in governmental decisions.
Step 3. What are your economic interests?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from certain enumerated economic inierests. Theii economic interests are described in section
87 I03 and Regulations I 8703- I g703.5, inclusive:


File No. A-10-176
Page No. 3
o I public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a
direct or indirect invesrment of $2,000 or more. (iection g7103(a); Regularion
18703.1(a).)
o d public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she is a
director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds aoy poritio., oir*og"."rr.
(Section 87103(d); Regulation f SZO3.f iUl.j
o d public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct
or indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section szio:@); Regulation 1g703.2.)
o An official has an economic interest in any source of income, including promised
income' totaling $500 or more within tz months prior to the decision. (section g7103(c);
Regulation 13703.3.)
o d public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts
total $420 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. ("Section sTro:("1; Regulation
t87O3.4.)
o I public official has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses, income, assets,
or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediate family. rnis is commonly referred
to as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section g7103; rt.egulation iszol.s.)
The only economic interest suggested by your account of the facts is an interest in real
property' You may have a potential economic interest in the leasehold inter"ri'in tr,. hangar you
lease' Under the Act, an interest in real property i""iud.. a leasehold interest in real property
within the official's jurisdiction. (Section azolz).However, the terms ,.interest in real property,,
and "leasehold interest," as used in the Act, do not includeih. in,"r.r, or u i*urt in a periodic
tenancy of one month orless. (Regulation 18233.)Accordingly, the rrorgu, you rent is not
considered an economic interest in real property for puqposes of this analysis. we conclude our
analysis here because the Act's conflict of inteiert p-ui"ions apply only when a public official
has an economic interest in a govemmental a""iSol.t 
'"^"'
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
2 You ofrer no facts to suggest the decision will afrect the cost of your lease. If it does, you may have a
conflict of interest base on personar finances and you shourd seek f'urther advice.

sections.question_synthetic: May a Board Director of an airport district participate in decisions regarding the District's purchase of county-owned property adjacent to the airport when the director leases a hangar from the District within 500 feet of the property boundary?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict of interest exists under the Political Reform Act because a month-to-month lease of a hangar is not considered an interest in real property for purposes of the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; Skipped (too short): conclusion, question; QUESTION has only 0 words; CONCLUSION has only 0 words; LLM: Document contains OCR artifacts (e.g., 'pRACrrcES', 'i-n which') but content is legible. QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are labeled but empty/minimal; full question and conclusion text extracted from 'Re:', 'Your Request for Advice', and 'CONCLUSION' subsections. Eight-step conflict analysis framework applied. Footnote 2 indicates potential future conflict if lease costs are affected.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a Board Director of an airport district participate in decisions regarding the District's purchase of county-owned property adjacent to the airport when the director leases a hangar from the District within 500 feet of the property boundary?

CONCLUSION: No conflict of interest exists under the Political Reform Act because a month-to-month lease of a hangar is not considered an interest in real property for purposes of the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter concluding that a director of an airport district may participate in decisions regarding the district's purchase of county-owned property, as the month-to-month hangar lease the director holds does not constitute a disqualifying economic interest under the Political Reform Act.
